entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 5
entries.excerpt: Having numbered the generations in the line of Cain and ended the account of Lamech’s words to his wives, Moses turns to the numbering of the generations in the line of Seth and, beginning with Adam, says: “And Adam lived a hundred and thirty years, and begat a son in his own likeness, after his image” (Gen 5:3). In Seth, who was in every way like Adam, is presented to us the likeness of the Son of God, who is the image of the Father who begot Him — as Seth is the image of Adam who begot him.…
